description: A word refuses to arrive on time; 'nuo' keeps the floor while the speaker waits for it. This audible thinking pause works at the start of a turn or midway through one. Unlike 'mio' (mmhm), it comes from the current speaker rather than the listener.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let air pass through the nose as the tongue draws back and the lips round for high-back 'u'. Lower the tongue slightly into mid-back 'o' for the second syllable and relax the rounding. Stress 'nu'.
usage_notes: Either vowel may be prolonged to match the pause. The interjection can also stand alone while a response takes shape.
